element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Introduced against basic street gang members in controlled arena space
Available after stunning enemies with basic attacks; escalates combat depth
Double-tap movement to run, then execute directional attack variations
Press down on fallen enemy to sit and pummel with front attack button
First boss only vulnerable to collar grab and throw when health is sufficiently depleted
Resource management across stages; limited availability
Ultimate test combining all learned mechanics with high aggression patterns

typical_relief_triggers: Clearing all enemies from current arena section
typical_tension_triggers: Boss character's invulnerability phases (before health depletes sufficiently)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating difficulty across four missions with short relief intervals between enemy waves; boss encounters punctuate each stage with heightened tension peaks

difficulty_curve.early_game: Mission 1 introduces basic gang members and foundational combat mechanics in Shinjuku Station (JP) / subway (US); relatively forgiving enemy patterns to establish core skills
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Missions 2-3 introduce denser enemy formations, more aggressive attacks, and boss characters requiring specific mastery of grab/throw mechanics; health management becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Mission 4 culminates with Sabu (yakuza boss) requiring accumulated mastery of all mechanics with sustained enemy pressure and minimal recovery opportunities
notable_spikes: First boss encounter (Riki/Jack) - introduces boss invulnerability mechanic requiring strategic health depletion before grab availability
Mission 3 bar section - increased enemy density and speed
Final boss Sabu - combines high aggression with mechanics from all previous bosses

notable_level_patterns.description: Each mission presents isometric/three-quarter perspective arena spaces where player moves horizontally and vertically; enemies attack in waves within bounded area before advancing to next arena section
Each mission culminates in single boss encounter that synthesizes mechanics introduced within that stage; boss requires health depletion before grab/throw mechanics become viable
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All four missions end with boss battles (Riki, Shinji, Misuzu, Sabu)

notable_level_patterns.description: Despite isometric movement in four directions (up/down/left/right), combat system limited to forward and backward directional attacks; creates tactical positioning requirement
